mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rising rate periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >55% of instances when unemployment rises rapidly, invalidating the labor deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure that erodes real consumer purchasing power, forcing central banks to tighten policy and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint acceleration in CPI and deceleration in retail sales fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate capacity utilization, which signals immediate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in >65% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.5%, monetary policy becomes highly accommodative, lowering borrowing costs for corporations and stimulating risk appetite, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in >60% of periods when the federal funds rate is below 1.5%, breaking the low-rate risk premium link.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic expansion and rising household income, which supports corporate profit growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day SPX returns, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and real GDP growth signal synchronized strength in consumer demand and macroeconomic output, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising housing starts and GDP growth predicts negative SPX returns in >55% of cases, negating the demand synchronization effect.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production indicate a contraction in the supply sector and weakening business investment, which triggers immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
